best.explanation: Belgas looks like a fun-sized north coast window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 65 kJ, 12 s period, lully 19/100, and wind is the main risk.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot; gusts over 35 km/h could make it messy.

ranked.explanation: Almagreira looks like a fun-sized north coast window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 45 kJ, 5 s period, lully 10/100, and wind is the main risk.  An exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal; this is best for small, clean north-coast days when protected spots look underpowered, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot; 6-9s at high tide is usually too fat for exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia; gusts over 35 km/h could make it messy.

ranked.explanation: Lagide looks like a fun-sized window for Fun progression, Low confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 49 kJ, 12 s period, lully 10/100, and wind is the main risk.  A dependable left-hand reef that turns modest swell into real rides; this is best for clean, playful walls when the bigger beaches are too wild or too small; 10-13s at high tide is softer but still workable on the reef shelves.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot; gusts over 35 km/h could make it messy.

ranked.explanation: Supertubos looks like a small window for Worth checking, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 33 kJ, 12 s period, lully 6/100, and wind is not fully clear.  A fast, hollow sand-bottom wave that gets serious quickly; this is best for short, punchy bodyboard barrels when it is small enough for comfort.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; 10-13s at high tide can get too backed up for Supertubos / Molhe Leste; gusts over 35 km/h could make it messy.
